questions.question:
Which of the following parts of our body help us in movement?

  1. Bones
  2. Skin
  3. Muscles
  4. Organs

Choose the correct answer from the option below.
Which of the following joints is immovable?
Which of the following organisms does not have both muscles and skeleton for movement?
Underwater divers wear fin-like flippers on their feet to

options_data.option: walk over the bottom of the sea (sea bed).

questions.question: Snail moves with the help of

options_data.explanation: Animals that do not have a backbone are known as invertebrates. A snail is an invertebrate. The foot of a snail is made of strong muscles, which produces a slimy substance called mucous. A snail moves using its muscular foot.

questions.question: How many muscle works together to move a bone?

options_data.explanation: A pair of bones move together to move a bone as biceps and triceps in case of arm movement. Pair is needed to move to and fro motion of the bone

questions.question: Name the type of joint of your hand which helps you to grasp a badminton racket.

solution_data.solution: The joints in our hand are hinge joints which help us grasp a badminton racket.

questions.question: What would have happened if our backbone was made up of one single bone?

solution_data.solution: If the backbone was made up of a single bone instead of 33 small vertebras, we were not able to move the body in the desired direction. The flexibility of the backbone is due to the vertebra.

questions.question: Joint which allows movement in all directions provide the one-word answer to the statement:

solution_data.solution: Ball and socket joint - Joint which allows movement in all directions.

questions.question: Hard structure that forms the skeleton provide a one-word answer to the statement.

solution_data.solution: Bones - Hard structure that forms the skeleton.

questions.question: Part of the body with a fixed joint provide a one-word answer to the statement.

solution_data.solution: Upper jaw with skull- Part of the body with a fixed joint.

questions.question: Help in the movement of the body by contraction and relaxation provide one word answers to the statement.

solution_data.solution: Muscles help in the movement of the body by contraction and relaxation.

questions.question: Bones that join with chest bone at one end and to the backbone at the other end provide a one-word answer to the statement.

solution_data.solution: Ribs - Bones that join with chest bone at one end and to the backbone at the other end.

questions.question: Framework of bones which gives shape to our body provide a one-word answer to the statement.

solution_data.solution: The framework of bones which gives shape to our body is known as Skeleton.

questions.question: Bones that enclose the organs of our body that lie below the abdomen provide a one-word answer to the statement.

solution_data.solution: Pelvic bones - Bones that enclose the organs of our body that lie below the abdomen.

questions.question: Joint where our neck joins the head provide a one-word answer to the statement.

solution_data.solution: The joint where our neck joins the head is called as a pivot joint.

questions.question: Part of the skeleton that forms the earlobe provide a one-word answer to the statement.

solution_data.solution: The parts of the skeleton that form the earlobe is cartilage.

questions.question: Write the type of joint which is used for the movement: A cricket bowler bowls the ball.

solution_data.solution: When the cricket bowler bowls the ball it involves the shoulder joint, i.e. Ball and socket joint.

questions.question: Write the type of joint which is used for the movement: A girl moves her head in right and left direction.

solution_data.solution: It involves a pivot joint that connects our head to the neck.

questions.question: Write the type of joint which is used for the movement: A person lifts weights to build up his biceps.

solution_data.solution: The hinge joint is used by a person to lifts weights to build up his biceps.

questions.question:
Match the name of the animals given in Column 'I' with its body parts used for movement given in Column 'II'.

Column I

Column II

(a) Human being

(i) Fins

(b) Cow

(ii) Wings

(c) Snake

(iii) Legs

(d) Eagle

(iv) Whole body

(e) Fish

(v) Limbs

solution_data.solution:
1. Human being - (iii) Legs
  2. Cow - (v) Limbs
  3. Snake - (iv) Whole body
  4. Eagle - (ii) Wings
  5. Fish - (i) Fins

questions.question:
Given below is a list of different types of movements in animals.
Running, jumping, Walking, Slithering, Crawling, Flying, Swimming, Creeping
Write the types of movements seen in each animal.

  1. Duck
  2. Horse
  3. Kangaroo
  4. Snail
  5. Snake
  6. Fish
  7. Human beings
  8. Cockroach

solution_data.solution:
The types of movements seen in each animal are as follows:

  1. Duck - Flying, walking, and swimming
  2. Horse - Running and walking
  3. Kangaroo - Jumping and walking
  4. Snail - Creeping
  5. Snake - Slithering
  6. Fish - Swimming
  7. Human being - Walking and running
  8. Cockroach - Walking and flying (short distances)

questions.question: Boojho fell off a tree and hurt his ankle. On examination, the doctor confirmed that the ankle was fractured. How was it detected?

solution_data.solution:
The doctor confirmed that the ankle was fractured can be detected by the following ways:

  1. If the doctor finds a swollen region around his injured region he can confirm that it is a fracture.
  2. The doctor can feel the fracture running his hand on the injured regions.
  3. The doctor can examine through the X-ray test.

questions.question: Bones are hard structures and cannot be bent. But, we can still bend our elbow, knee, etc. How is this possible?

solution_data.solution: Elbow and knee are not made up of a single bone, but two or more bones that are joined to each other by a joint (Hinge joint). This joint along with the muscles help us to bend the elbow and knee.

questions.question: Which type of movement would have been possible if our elbow had a fixed joint?

solution_data.solution: If our elbow had a fixed joint, then we would not be able to freely move our elbow the way we are doing.

questions.question: Which type of movement would have been possible if we were to have a ball and socket joint between our neck and head?

solution_data.solution: A ball and socket joint is a type of synovial joint which allows the movement in all direction in a circle. This joint is found in the shoulder. If we have a ball and socket joint between our neck and head then the movement of the head will take place in all directions in a circle instead of moving forward-backward and left-right direction (Pivotal joint).

questions.question: Earthworms are known as 'farmer's friends'. Why?

solution_data.solution: Earthworm moves through the soil that loosens and aerates the soil. This also helps the water to reach the roots quickly. Earthworm's excreta increases the fertility of the soil. After their death, they get decomposed and add humus to the soil. So, Earthworms are called farmers friends.

The bones are moved by alternate ______(f)______ and ______(g)______ of two sets of ______(h)______ attached to them.

solution_data.solution:
1.

    2. movement
    3. contraction

    8. Muscles

questions.question: How is the skeleton of a bird well-suited for flying?

solution_data.solution:
The skeleton of a bird is well suited for flying because

  1. The skeleton in a bird is strong but the bones are light in weight and hollow inside.
  2. The fore-limbs in a bird are modified into wings. Wings are supported with strong muscles.
  3. The hind-limbs or the legs are with a claw which is modified to hold the support for perching.
  4. The body is light in weight and streamlined- narrow in front and at the back, broad in the middle which reduces resistance from the air.

Birds fly by flapping their wings. when a bird is in flight it keeps its hind-limbs close to its body. While landing, it brings out the hind-limbs and keeps the wings open and stationary. Folding of the limbs while in the air reduces resistance from the air.

questions.question: In Fig. there are two snakes of the same size slithering on the sand. Can you identify which of them would move faster and why?

solution_data.solution: Snakes do not have limbs but have rough scales on the underside of the body that help them in their locomotion. A snake form loops in its body while slithering. Each loop of the snake gives it a forward push by pressing against the ground. The snake with a large number of loops moves much faster than the snake with less number of loops. Thus snake “A” will move faster than snake “B”.
